back_translation: Then the whole congregation of the sons of Israel assembled at Shiloh, and they pitched there the Tent of Meeting. And the land had been subdued before them.
Yet there remained among the sons of Israel those who had not yet divided their inheritance — seven tribes.
And Joshua said to the sons of Israel, 'How long are you slack about coming to take possession of the land which the LORD, the God of your fathers, has given you?
Give yourselves three men per tribe, and I will send them. They shall arise and walk through the land, and shall write it down according to their inheritance, and come back to me.
They shall divide it into seven portions. Judah shall stand on his border in the south, and the house of Joseph shall stand on their border in the north.
And you shall write the land in seven portions and bring it here to me, and I will cast a lot for you here before the LORD our God.

For there is no portion for the Levites among you, because the priesthood of the LORD is their inheritance. And Gad and Reuben and the half-tribe of Manasseh have received their inheritance beyond the Jordan to the east, which Moses the servant of the LORD gave them.'

So the men arose and went, and Joshua commanded those who went to write the land, saying, 'Go and walk through the land, and write it, and return to me; and here I will cast a lot for you before the LORD at Shiloh.'
So the men went and passed through the land and wrote it down by cities, in seven portions, on a document. And they came to Joshua at the camp at Shiloh.
And Joshua cast a lot for them at Shiloh before the LORD, and Joshua there divided the land for the sons of Israel according to their divisions.
The lot of the tribe of the sons of Benjamin came up according to their clans, and the border of their lot came out between the sons of Judah and the sons of Joseph.

Their border on the north side was from the Jordan, and the border went up to the northern slope of Jericho, and went up in the hill country westward, and its outgoings were at the wilderness of Beth-aven.
From there the border crossed to Luz, to the southern slope of Luz (that is, Bethel); and the border went down to Ataroth-addar, on the hill which is south of Lower Beth-horon.

And the border curved and turned around to the western side, southward from the hill which faces Beth-horon southward; and its outgoings were at Kiriath-baal (that is, Kiriath-jearim), a city of the sons of Judah. This was the western side.
And the southern side was from the edge of Kiriath-jearim; the border went out westward, and went out to the spring of the Waters of Nephtoah.

And the border went down to the edge of the hill which faces the Valley of Ben-hinnom, which is in the Valley of Rephaim to the north, and went down the Valley of Hinnom to the southern slope of the Jebusite, and went down to En-rogel.
And it curved northward and went out to En-shemesh, and went out to Geliloth, which is opposite the Ascent of Adummim, and went down to the Stone of Bohan son of Reuben.
And it passed to the slope opposite the Arabah northward, and went down to the Arabah.
And the border passed to the northern slope of Beth-hoglah, and the outgoings of the border were at the northern bay of the Salt Sea, at the southern edge of the Jordan. This was the southern border.
And the Jordan was the border on the east side. This was the inheritance of the sons of Benjamin by its borders all around, according to their clans.
And the cities of the tribe of the sons of Benjamin according to their clans were: Jericho and Beth-hoglah and Emek-keziz,
and Beth-arabah and Zemaraim and Bethel,
and Avvim and Parah and Ophrah,
and Chephar-ammoni and Ophni and Geba — twelve cities with their villages.
Gibeon and Ramah and Beeroth,
and Mizpeh and Chephirah and Mozah,
and Rekem and Irpeel and Taralah,
and Zelah, Haeleph, and the Jebusite (that is, Jerusalem), Gibeah, Kiriath — fourteen cities with their villages. This was the inheritance of the sons of Benjamin according to their clans.
